name: Independent Code Review
description: Changes are reviewed by an independent code reviewer prior to code being merged and released to production. Independent code review restricts the ability for anyone individual from taking a change production without involving an independent party (4-eyes principle).

attributes.text: Code Reviews are performed prior to committing a change to the main branch
testable_criteria: Is there evidence of a code review for the change?
Does the timestamp of the code review completion predate the timestamp of the code commit or merge to the main branch?

attributes.text: Code Review approvals are performed by independent code reviewers
testable_criteria: Is there an explicit approval recorded on the code review?
Is the person who approved the code review someone other than the author of the code change?

attributes.text: Testing is performed in accordance with the testing policy
testable_criteria: Are test coverage reports generated and visible within the code review?
Does the coverage report show unit test line coverage >= 80% for all new code?
Does the coverage report show branch coverage >= 70%?
Does the coverage report show function coverage >= 80%?
IF the change touches critical integration points, is integration test coverage >= 60%?
IF the change touches security-sensitive, payment, or authentication flows, does the coverage report show 100% coverage?
IF the change modifies legacy code, is the previous coverage level maintained or improved?
Are test results visible in the pull request status checks?
Do the PR status checks show all automated unit tests passed with zero failures in a clean CI/CD environment prior to merge?
Do the PR status checks show all integration tests passed in a clean CI/CD environment prior to merge?
Do the PR status checks show security tests passed prior to release?
IF minimum coverage requirements are not met, does the change fall under an explicit policy exemption (e.g., Documentation-Only, Legacy Code Integration, Dependency Updates)?
